description:
You are an IT-support technician who has just pulled last-week’s raw ticket dump into
/home/user/helpdesk/tickets.csv

Your job is to:  

1. Inspect the raw CSV, eliminate duplicated tickets (same **TicketID**).  
2. Normalise every date to ISO-8601 form **YYYY-MM-DD**.  
     • The raw file mixes “DD/MM/YYYY”, “DD-MM-YYYY”, “YYYY/MM/DD” and “YYYY-MM-DD”.  
3. Sort the resulting, de-duplicated tickets in ascending numeric **TicketID** order.  
4. Save the cleaned result to  
   /home/user/helpdesk/processed/tickets_clean.csv  
5. Produce a short statistics log called  
   /home/user/helpdesk/processed/ticket_stats.log

Directory /home/user/helpdesk already exists and is writable; create the “processed” sub-directory yourself.

----------  Input file specification  ----------
The file /home/user/helpdesk/tickets.csv already contains a header line followed by the data.  
Columns: TicketID,User,Status,Priority,Date  
There are fewer than a dozen data rows, but some TicketID values are duplicated and the
date formats are mixed as described above.

----------  tickets_clean.csv – EXACT format ----------
• Must be plain UTF-8 text, UNIX line-feeds.  
• First line (header) must be exactly:
  TicketID,User,Status,Priority,Date
• Each subsequent line: the five comma-separated fields in the order shown.  
• Dates must all be YYYY-MM-DD.  
• No duplicate TicketID rows must remain.  
• Rows must appear in strictly ascending numeric TicketID order.  
• No blank lines or trailing spaces.

----------  ticket_stats.log – EXACT format ----------
ticket_stats.log must contain exactly six lines, each terminated with a UNIX line-feed, **no extra blank line at the end**.  
1. TotalTickets: <integer>  
2. UniqueUsers: <integer>  
3. OpenTickets: <integer>  
4. ClosedTickets: <integer>  
5. HighPriority: <integer>  
6. ReportGeneratedUTC: <timestamp>

Rules for the values:  
• “TotalTickets” = number of unique TicketID rows that appear in tickets_clean.csv.  
• “UniqueUsers” = number of distinct values in the “User” column after cleaning.  
• “OpenTickets”  = rows whose Status is exactly “Open”.  
• “ClosedTickets”= rows whose Status is exactly “Closed”.  
• “HighPriority” = rows whose Priority is exactly “High”.  
• “ReportGeneratedUTC” must be the current UTC time when you run the command,
  formatted exactly YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (the literal “Z” suffix).

Example of the required layout (values here are only illustrative):

TotalTickets: 42  
UniqueUsers: 17  
OpenTickets: 23  
ClosedTickets: 19  
HighPriority: 6  
ReportGeneratedUTC: 2023-09-18T13:05:27Z

----------  Completion criteria ----------
The automated checker will verify that:

• /home/user/helpdesk/processed/tickets_clean.csv exists and matches every rule above.  
• /home/user/helpdesk/processed/ticket_stats.log exists and its first five lines contain the correct numeric values, while the sixth line matches the timestamp pattern.  
• No extra files were created or modified outside /home/user/helpdesk or its “processed” sub-directory.  

Use any standard GNU/Linux command-line tools you like (awk, sed, sort, uniq, grep, cut, etc.) but do NOT hard-code the answers; derive them from the raw CSV. 5-10 commands in total is enough, but feel free to experiment in the shell.

When you are done, both output files must be readable by the current user.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent begins:

Path: /home/user/helpdesk/tickets.csv  
Contents (UTF-8, UNIX line-feeds):

TicketID,User,Status,Priority,Date
101,alice,Open,High,18/09/2023
102,bob,Closed,Low,18-09-2023
103,charlie,Open,Medium,2023/09/17
104,dave,Closed,High,2023-09-17
101,alice,Open,High,18/09/2023
105,alice,Closed,Low,2023-09-16
106,erin,Open,High,16-09-2023
107,frank,Open,Low,2023/09/15
106,erin,Open,High,16-09-2023

Expected cleaned file after successful completion:

Path: /home/user/helpdesk/processed/tickets_clean.csv  
Exact contents:

TicketID,User,Status,Priority,Date
101,alice,Open,High,2023-09-18
102,bob,Closed,Low,2023-09-18
103,charlie,Open,Medium,2023-09-17
104,dave,Closed,High,2023-09-17
105,alice,Closed,Low,2023-09-16
106,erin,Open,High,2023-09-16
107,frank,Open,Low,2023-09-15

Expected statistics:

TotalTickets: 7  
UniqueUsers: 6  
OpenTickets: 4  
ClosedTickets: 3  
HighPriority: 3  

Automated test will:
• compare tickets_clean.csv byte-for-byte against the block above;  
• read ticket_stats.log and check that lines 1-5 match exactly the five lines shown
  (numeric values), and that line 6 matches the regex  
  ^ReportGeneratedUTC: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

No other files or directories are required for verification.
